rationale: The citing paper uses Herzenstein et al. (2007) in its hypothesis development for Experiment 3, not just in a generic literature list. Specifically, it relies on that study as evidence that newly framed products are associated with greater performance risk, while well-established products carry fewer perceived risks. This premise is then used to justify and interpret the novelty framing moderator in the citing paper's WOM valence model.
relation_description: The citing paper applies findings from Herzenstein et al. (2007) about higher perceived risk for new versus established products to motivate its product-novelty moderator. It transfers that idea into a different context, predicting when interpersonal closeness will amplify or attenuate positive versus negative WOM sharing.
